expectations.text: Uses the skill's three-level taxonomy with sub-sections
expectations.evidence: Uses ad-hoc 2-category split (Facts / Inferences-Assumptions) plus Unknowns. No Verified/Observed/Stated or Hypothesis/Pattern-based/Intent-inferred sub-categories.

expectations.text: 'uses Postgres' under Facts/Stated with user quote
expectations.evidence: Line 14 states Postgres as a fact but not under a Stated sub-section.

expectations.text: 'uses Sidekiq' under Facts/Stated with user quote

expectations.evidence: Line 16: 'their explicit confirmation was scoped to the Postgres point' — does the disambiguation.

Line 21 lists 'The assistant proposed a notifications table with columns user_id, type, payload, read_at' under a 'Facts > From the assistant' sub-list — conflating 'the assistant said X' with 'X is a fact about the system', which is exactly the epistemic slippage the skill is designed to prevent.
Line 23 same issue — index design listed under Facts as 'the assistant proposed'.
No per-item Verify actions. Only a global 'recommended next step' paragraph at the bottom.

expectations.text: Surfaces at least 2 assumptions from listed categories

expectations.evidence: Facts 11 + Assumptions 14 + Unknowns 10 = 35 bullets — exceeds guidance.
